occurrence_sentence: Fermilab Leads Partnership to Create $115 million National Quantum Information Science Research Center to Build Revolutionary Quantum Computer

The U.S. Department of Energy's Fermilab has been selected to lead one of five national centers to bring about transformational advances in quantum information science as a part of the U.S. National Quantum Initiative, announced the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, the National Science Foundation and the U.S. Department of Energy today.
Fermilab leads partnership to create $115 million National Quantum Information Science Research Center to build revolutionary quantum computer.

occurrence_sentence: The SQMS Center is part of a $625 million federal program to facilitate and foster quantum innovation in the United States.

interaction_phrase: The 2018 National Quantum Initiative Act called for
occurrence_sentence: The 2018 National Quantum Initiative Act called for a long-term, large-scale commitment of U.S. scientific and technological resources to quantum science.

occurrence_sentence: The 2018 National Quantum Initiative Act called for a long-term, large-scale commitment of U.S. scientific and technological resources to quantum science.

interaction_phrase: SQMS Deputy Director James Sauls of Northwestern University
occurrence_sentence: "The breadth of the SQMS physics, materials science, device fabrication and characterization technology combined with the expertise in large-scale integration capabilities by the SQMS Center is unprecedented for superconducting quantum science and technology," said SQMS Deputy Director James Sauls of Northwestern University.

interaction_phrase: SQMS Deputy Director James Sauls of Northwestern University
occurrence_sentence: "The breadth of the SQMS physics, materials science, device fabrication and characterization technology combined with the expertise in large-scale integration capabilities by the SQMS Center is unprecedented for superconducting quantum science and technology," said SQMS Deputy Director James Sauls of Northwestern University.

interaction_phrase: combining them with Rigetti's state-of-the-art planar structures
occurrence_sentence: "We have the most coherent – by a factor of more than 200 – 3-D superconducting cavities in the world, which will be turned into quantum processors with unprecedented performance by combining them with Rigetti's state-of-the-art planar structures," said Fermilab scientist Alexander Romanenko, SQMS technology thrust leader and Fermilab SRF program manager.

"Now is the time to harness the strengths of the DOE laboratories and partners to identify the underlying mechanisms limiting quantum devices in order to push their performance to the next level for quantum computing and sensing applications," said SQMS Chief Engineer Matt Kramer, Ames Laboratory.

interaction_phrase: partnering to contribute world-class materials science and superconductivity expertise
occurrence_sentence: Northwestern University, Ames Laboratory, Fermilab, Rigetti Computing, the National Institute of Standards and Technology, the Italian National Institute for Nuclear Physics and several universities are partnering to contribute world-class materials science and superconductivity expertise to target sources of decoherence.

interaction_phrase: further the energy, economic and national security interests of the United States
occurrence_sentence: "By partnering with world-class experts, our work will translate ground-breaking science into scalable superconducting quantum computing systems and commercialize capabilities that will further the energy, economic and national security interests of the United States," said Rigetti Computing CEO Chad Rigetti.

interaction_phrase: successfully collaborating with Fermilab for more than 40 years
occurrence_sentence: "The Italian National Institute for Nuclear Physics has been successfully collaborating with Fermilab for more than 40 years and is excited to be a member of the extraordinary SQMS team," said INFN President Antonio Zoccoli.

occurrence_sentence: SQMS partner Rigetti Computing will provide crucial state-of-the-art qubit fabrication and full stack quantum computing capabilities required for building the SQMS quantum computer.
